name: Bangladesh

outbound_official.note: no official Bangladesh government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data

visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.purpose: For visitors travelling to Bangladesh for tourism, casual visits to friends/relatives, seminars, conferences, workshops, study tours, or religious purposes. No commercial activities permitted.
visa_types.notes: Standard issuance is single entry for up to 3 months. Double and multiple-entry options available for certain nationalities at different rates. Fees vary by nationality based on reciprocity (e.g. UK £105 single, USA £146). Validity begins from date of issue, not travel date. Extension up to 1 additional month possible through Department of Immigration and Passport (DIP) in Bangladesh. Application form must be completed online, printed, and submitted physically to a Bangladesh mission.

visa_types.name: Business Visa
visa_types.purpose: For businesspersons visiting Bangladesh for the purpose of trade, commerce, and investment. Requires invitation from a recognised Bangladeshi business organisation.
visa_types.notes: Maximum validity 1 year; maximum stay 60 days per visit. Single, double, or multiple entry options available. Extension up to 3 years (max 6-month stays per visit) obtainable from DIP. Fees vary by nationality (UK £105 single/double, USA £146). Online application form must be printed and submitted physically to a Bangladesh mission.

visa_types.name: Work Permit / Employment Visa
visa_types.purpose: For experts, advisers, and employees working in government, semi-government, autonomous bodies, or industrial/commercial organisations in Bangladesh, or conducting unpaid work with registered NGOs.
visa_types.notes: Initial visa valid for maximum 3 months / 90 days. Single, double, or multiple entry options available. Extensions up to 3 years obtainable through DIP. Requires employer letter, appointment letter, contract with salary details, and Ministry/BOI/BEPZA recommendation. Work permit (separate from visa) must be applied for within 15 days of arrival through BIDA OSS; cost BDT 5,000 per year. Fees vary by nationality (UK £105 single, USA £146).

visa_types.name: Student Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals enrolled at a recognised educational institution in Bangladesh.
visa_types.notes: Issued for maximum 1 year with multiple entries. Max stay per visit depends on educational institution requirements. Extension up to course tenure obtainable from DIP. Requires invitation/declaration letter from Bangladeshi educational institution and bank guarantee documents. Fees vary by nationality (UK £105 single, USA £146).

visa_types.name: Investor Visa (P1 Visa)
visa_types.purpose: For foreign investors in private sector industrial or commercial entities seeking to establish or manage businesses in Bangladesh through joint ventures or 100% foreign ownership.
visa_types.notes: Also referred to as 'P1 Visa' in BIDA documentation. Issued for maximum 1 year; single, double, or multiple entries. Extensions up to 5 years obtainable from DIP. Requires BOI/BEPZA/BIDA recommendation letter. Minimum investment of USD 50,000 required for branch/liaison/representative office registration. BIDA OSS processes visa recommendations within 24 hours with complete documentation. Fees vary by nationality (UK £105 single, USA £146).

visa_types.name: E Visa (Employee Visa via BIDA)
visa_types.purpose: For employees of private or public organisations (foreign and local companies) working in Bangladesh, processed through BIDA One Stop Service.
visa_types.notes: Separate from general employment visa; specifically for employees of companies registered/operating in Bangladesh. Applied for through BIDA OSS (bidaquickserv.org). Work permit must be obtained within 15 days of arrival. Validity and fee details not published on official portal; contact BIDA directly.

visa_types.name: E1 Visa (Machinery/Software Installation Visa via BIDA)
visa_types.purpose: For persons engaged with machinery and software installation, project inspection, or related technical services in Bangladesh.
visa_types.notes: Processed through BIDA One Stop Service (OSS). Specific validity, fees, and stay limits not published on official portal; applicants should contact BIDA at +880-2-44826795-99 or info@bida.gov.bd.

visa_types.name: NGO Visa
visa_types.purpose: For individuals appointed to work in NGOs registered in Bangladesh, whether paid or unpaid roles with Bureau of NGO Affairs-registered organisations.
visa_types.notes: Maximum 3 months per issuance; extensions up to 3 years from DIP. Single, double, or multiple entries available. Requires NGO Affairs Bureau recommendation letter and appointment letter with full terms and salary details. Fees vary by nationality (UK £105 single, USA £146).

visa_types.name: Research Visa
visa_types.purpose: For individuals participating in research, training, or internship programs at government-approved agencies or institutions in Bangladesh.
visa_types.notes: Maximum 3 months validity; extensions up to 3 years from DIP. Requires letters from both host and home institutions, and consent letter from relevant Bangladesh Ministry. Fees vary by nationality (UK £105 single, USA £146).

visa_types.name: Journalist Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign journalists of newspapers, magazines, TV or radio networks, or news agencies seeking to collect information or fulfill official reporting duties in Bangladesh.
visa_types.notes: Maximum 1 month per issuance; extensions up to 3 years from DIP. May require additional approval from Bangladesh authority beyond standard 7 working days. Requires press card, FF-I and FF-II forms, equipment list, invitation letter, and employer letter. Fees vary by nationality (UK £105 single, USA £146).

visa_types.name: Intern Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals undertaking internship programs at organisations in Bangladesh.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a distinct category by Bangladesh High Commission London. Specific validity, max stay, fees, and entry details not published on the official page. Contact relevant Bangladesh mission for details.

visa_types.name: Transit Visa
visa_types.purpose: For foreign nationals transiting through Bangladesh en route to a third country.
visa_types.notes: Listed as a distinct visa category by Bangladesh High Commission London. Specific validity period, max transit stay, fees, and entry details not accessible on the official portal pages during research. Contact relevant Bangladesh mission for details.

visa_types.name: Visa on Arrival
visa_types.purpose: For tourism, business, investment, or government job purposes; issued at designated ports of entry in Bangladesh to eligible nationality holders.
visa_types.notes: Fee of USD 50 paid in cash at Sonali Bank PLC counter at port of entry. Eligible nationalities: US, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Russia, China, Japan, Singapore, Malaysia, South Korea, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, Bahrain, Egypt, Turkey, Brunei, and all European countries. Also available to: citizens of countries with no Bangladesh embassy, foreign embassy officials and UN staff based in Bangladesh, persons of Bangladeshi origin (non-SAARC), and investors with BIDA/BEPZA investment certificate. Issuance is at the sole discretion of the immigration officer and is not guaranteed. Extension beyond 30 days possible through DIP but described as difficult.

visa_types.name: Government Delegates / Diplomatic / Official Visa
visa_types.purpose: For government delegates, diplomatic personnel, other embassy officials, and staff of UN or international organisations travelling to Bangladesh on official duties.
visa_types.notes: Listed as distinct categories by Bangladesh High Commission London: 'Government Delegates Visa', 'Diplomatic/other Embassy officials Visa', and 'UN and other International Organizations visa'. Holders of diplomatic/official passports from approximately 32 countries may be exempt from visa requirements under bilateral agreements. Full list of bilateral visa-exemption countries is not published on a currently accessible official Bangladesh government domain.

visa_on_arrival.notes: Visa on Arrival available for tourism, business, investment, or government job purposes. Fee: USD 50 paid in cash to Sonali Bank PLC counter at port of entry. Single entry. Issuance at discretion of immigration officer.

All European country nationals are eligible for Visa on Arrival. The official VOA portal states 'European countries' collectively without enumerating individual states. Fee: USD 50 paid in cash. Single entry. Issuance at discretion of immigration officer.

visa_on_arrival.nationality: Countries with no Bangladesh embassy / special operational categories
visa_on_arrival.notes: Citizens of countries where Bangladesh has no embassy can receive a Visa on Arrival. Also applicable if a person arrives from a third country (not their own) where there is no Bangladesh embassy. Officials and staff of foreign embassies and UN offices in Bangladesh can also receive On-Arrival Visas. Foreign citizens of Bangladeshi origin and their spouses/children also eligible. Foreign investors with BIDA/BEPZA certificate of investment can also obtain VoA with advance notification to DIP.

visa_policy.notes: Bangladesh's VOA-eligible country list is published on the official Special Branch portal (voa.specialbranch.gov.bd), operated by Bangladesh Police Special Branch. The e_visa section has been cleared: visa.gov.bd (the MRV portal) is NOT a true electronic visa - it is an online form-filling portal where applicants print the completed form and submit it physically to a Bangladesh mission. No electronically-issued or electronically-delivered visa exists. The NVR (No Visa Required) endorsement is a status-based category for persons of Bangladeshi origin and qualifying investors - not a nationality-based visa-free arrangement. SAARC country nationals are explicitly excluded from NVR. Bangladesh does not have a traditional visa-free arrangement for ordinary passport holders from any nationality. Bangladesh is a SAARC member and participates in the SAARC Visa Exemption Scheme (SVES) for 24 specified categories of dignitaries/officials. VoA issuance is at the sole discretion of the immigration officer at the port of entry and is not guaranteed.

eligible_nationalities: Foreign nationals of Bangladeshi origin (non-SAARC countries)
conditional_access.conditions: NVR (No Visa Required) endorsement is available to foreign citizens of Bangladeshi origin (excluding SAARC country nationals), their spouses, and children. Also available to spouses and children of Bangladeshi citizens holding foreign passports (non-SAARC). NVR is valid as long as the passport remains valid; unlimited stay duration within passport validity; multiple entries allowed. Applicants must present a Bangladeshi passport, 17-digit online verifiable Bangladeshi Birth Certificate, or National ID card as proof of Bangladeshi origin. SAARC country nationals are explicitly excluded. Application fee: USD 88 (including surcharge) payable by Money Order/Cashier Check.

NVR (No Visa Required) endorsement is also available to foreign nationals of any nationality who have invested a minimum of USD 5,000,000 (five million US dollars) in heavy industry or long-term investment in any industry/business in Bangladesh. Applicant must submit a document issued by BIDA, BEPZA, or BEZA confirming the qualifying investment. NVR valid for the life of the passport on which it is affixed; unlimited entries and unlimited stay duration within passport validity.

cbi.notes: No formal, officially published Citizenship-by-Investment (CBI) program was found on any official Bangladesh government domain (investbangladesh.gov.bd, dip.gov.bd, mofa.gov.bd). Third-party sources reference provisions in the Citizenship Act 1951 and National Industrial Policy 2022, but no official program page, application form, fee schedule, or formal program name was located on official government sources. Per the hard rules of this dataset, investment-linked citizenship thresholds cited only on non-official aggregators are not recorded.

programs.name: Investor Visa (P1-Visa) via BIDA
programs.notes: Foreign private investors in industrial and commercial enterprises can obtain a 'P1' (PI) visa on BIDA recommendation. Investors must remit a minimum of USD 50,000 as initial establishment cost (encashment certificate required) to register a branch, liaison, representative, or 100% foreign-owned company in Bangladesh. BIDA provides visa recommendations online through BIDA OSS (bidaquickserv.org) within 24 hours if documentation is complete. The P1 visa allows the investor to reside in Bangladesh while conducting business. Note: bida.gov.bd now permanently redirects (HTTP 301) to investbangladesh.gov.bd. Long-term residence / permanent residency thresholds for investors are referenced in other contexts but no official BIDA PR-specific threshold page was accessible. Work permits for expatriates cost BDT 5,000 per individual per year.

agreements: SAARC Visa Exemption Scheme (limited to 24 specified dignitaries/official categories)

validation_notes: CORRECTION: Removed the e_visa entry for 'All nationalities (online visa application - MRV system)'. The visa.gov.bd MRV portal is confirmed NOT a true e-visa: it is an online form-completion portal that requires physical printing and submission to a Bangladesh mission. No electronically-issued or electronically-delivered visa exists. This entry was misclassified; it has been removed from visa_policy.e_visa.

ADDITION: Added a second NVR (No Visa Required) conditional_access entry for foreign investors with USD 5,000,000 minimum investment in Bangladesh industry. This category is confirmed by multiple official Bangladesh embassy and consulate pages (Washington DC, Copenhagen, Brussels, Los Angeles, Toronto) - confirmed by Web search returning these official sources. The prior record only captured the Bangladeshi-origin NVR category and omitted the investor NVR category.
CORRECTION: Updated rbi[0].official_url from 'https://bida.gov.bd/faq' to 'https://investbangladesh.gov.bd/faq' as bida.gov.bd now permanently redirects (HTTP 301) to investbangladesh.gov.bd.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: VOA country list (US, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Russia, China, Japan, Singapore, Malaysia, South Korea, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, Bahrain, Egypt, Turkey, Brunei, European countries) - confirmed unchanged by direct Firecrawl scrape of voa.specialbranch.gov.bd/public/voa-instructions.html.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: VOA fee of USD 50 paid in cash at Sonali Bank PLC counter - confirmed unchanged.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: NVR Bangladeshi-origin category - confirmed unchanged including SAARC exclusion.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: No CBI program on any official source - confirmed no change.
NOTE: NVR application fee confirmed at USD 88 (including surcharge) per Bangladesh Washington embassy - added to the NVR Bangladeshi-origin conditional_access conditions.

NOTE: Bangladesh-Pakistan visa exemption agreement approved by Advisory Council (August 2025) covers diplomatic and official passport holders of both countries. This pertains to Bangladeshi outbound travel AND Pakistani diplomatic/official passport holders entering Bangladesh. However, the inbound list of 32+ countries whose diplomatic/official passport holders may enter Bangladesh visa-free cannot be confirmed from a currently accessible *.gov.bd page and remains a gap per official-source-first rules.

ADDITION (2026-06-20): Added visa_types array with 14 visa categories sourced from Bangladesh High Commission London (bhclondon.org.uk) and BIDA/investbangladesh.gov.bd. Visa types documented: Tourist, Business, Work/Employment, Student, Investor (P1), E Visa (BIDA employee), E1 Visa (BIDA machinery/software), NGO, Research, Journalist, Intern, Transit, Visa on Arrival, and Diplomatic/Government Delegates.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED (2026-06-27): Re-fetched voa.specialbranch.gov.bd/public/voa-instructions.html and voa.specialbranch.gov.bd/public/voa-info.html via WebFetch. VOA country list confirmed unchanged: US, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Russian Federation, China, Japan, Singapore, Malaysia, South Korea, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, Bahrain, Egypt, Turkey, Brunei, and European countries (collectively). Fee USD 50, max stay 30 days, single entry. UK is not listed separately - it falls under 'European countries'. All visa_on_arrival entries confirmed accurate.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED (2026-06-27): visa.gov.bd MRV portal re-fetched - confirmed still NOT a true e-visa; requires physical printing and submission to Bangladesh mission. e_visa array correctly remains empty.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED (2026-06-27): NVR investor threshold confirmed at USD 5,000,000 via WebSearch returning multiple official mofa.gov.bd embassy pages (Bangkok, Port Louis, Jakarta, Washington, Toronto, Los Angeles, Copenhagen). conditional_access investor NVR entry confirmed accurate.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED (2026-06-27): No new visa-free grants, ETA, or e-visa programs found on any official Bangladesh government domain. visa_free and eta arrays correctly remain empty.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): Re-fetched voa.specialbranch.gov.bd/public/voa-instructions.html and voa.specialbranch.gov.bd/public/voa-info.html directly via WebFetch. VOA country list confirmed unchanged: US, Canada, Australia, New Zealand, Russian Federation, China, Japan, Singapore, Malaysia, South Korea, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, Bahrain, Egypt, Turkey, Brunei, and European countries (collectively). Fee USD 50, max stay 30 days, single entry — all unchanged. No new countries added to VOA list.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): visa.gov.bd MRV portal re-confirmed NOT a true e-visa; requires physical printing and submission to Bangladesh mission. e_visa array correctly remains empty.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): NVR investor threshold of USD 5,000,000 re-confirmed via WebSearch returning official mofa.gov.bd embassy pages (Washington, Toronto, Los Angeles, Bangkok, Ottawa, Copenhagen, Istanbul, Jakarta). conditional_access investor NVR entry confirmed accurate.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): investbangladesh.gov.bd/faq re-fetched. P1 investor visa process confirmed unchanged; minimum USD 50,000 establishment cost for branch/liaison/representative office registration confirmed. No new programs found.

CONFIRMED UNCHANGED (2026-06-29): No new visa-free grants, ETA, or e-visa programs found on any official Bangladesh government domain. visa_free and eta arrays correctly remain empty. No policy changes detected for the 2025-2026 period.
gaps: NVR (No Visa Required) full eligibility details: Most mofa.gov.bd portal static pages (Toronto, LA, Brussels, Copenhagen, Washington static pages) are JavaScript-rendered and their body content is not directly extractable. Investor NVR threshold of USD 5M confirmed via WebSearch returning official Bangladesh embassy pages (Bangkok, Port Louis, Jakarta, Toronto, Washington, Los Angeles, Copenhagen) on mofa.gov.bd - re-confirmed 2026-06-27. Direct page body extraction still not possible due to SSL/rendering issues.

The list of European countries eligible for VoA is stated collectively as 'European countries' on the official source (voa.specialbranch.gov.bd); specific member state enumeration is not provided on the official portal.
Permanent residency minimum investment threshold: investbangladesh.gov.bd FAQ confirms P1 visa process but does not specify a PR-specific minimum investment threshold.
No formal CBI program with an official government page, program name, or application portal was found.
No digital nomad, startup, or talent fast-track scheme was found on any official Bangladesh government domain.
SAARC Visa Exemption Scheme details (full category list, stay duration per category) could not be confirmed from a Bangladesh government domain.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS - Credential-based: IATA Timatic and aggregators claim Bangladesh grants VoA to holders of valid US, UK, Canadian, Australian, or Schengen visas/residence permits. The official VoA portal (voa.specialbranch.gov.bd) does NOT mention any such rule. No official Bangladesh *.gov.bd domain was found that explicitly states this. Not recorded per official-source-first rules.

CONDITIONAL ACCESS - Diplomatic/service/official passport waivers: Bangladesh has signed bilateral visa exemption agreements for diplomatic/official passport holders with approximately 32 countries (confirmed by Prothom Alo citing official sources, and by bssnews.net reporting on Advisory Council approvals). Countries reported include China, Japan, South Korea, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Turkey, India, Maldives, Sri Lanka, Brunei, Cambodia, Indonesia, Laos, Malaysia, Myanmar, Philippines, Singapore, Thailand, Vietnam, Timor-Leste, Pakistan, Kazakhstan, Kosovo, Russia, Belarus, Serbia, Botswana, Gambia, Brazil, Chile, and Argentina. However, no currently accessible official Bangladesh *.gov.bd page publishes the complete country-by-country inbound exemption list in extractable form. Not recorded per official-source-first rules.
mofa.gov.bd portal subpages are JavaScript-rendered and return only navigation HTML during scraping; NVR and diplomatic visa details from these pages could not be extracted directly.

OUTBOUND OFFICIAL: No official Bangladesh government origin-side page equivalent to India's mea.gov.in/vffin was found. Neither mofa.gov.bd, dip.gov.bd, nor passport.gov.bd publishes an authoritative list of countries where Bangladesh ordinary passport holders can travel without a prior consular visa. All outbound visa-free data for Bangladeshi citizens available online originates from third-party aggregators (Henley, IATA Timatic, etc.). outbound_official.found is therefore set to false.
Transit Visa: Specific validity, max stay, fee, and entry details for the transit visa could not be extracted from official Bangladesh government pages during research.
Medical visa: Listed as a distinct category in some contexts but not found as a separately published page on official Bangladesh mission sites during this research session.
Family visa: Not listed as a distinct category on official Bangladesh mission pages reviewed; may be subsumed under tourist visa for visitors to relatives.
Tablig-Jamaat Visa: Listed as a category by BHC London but no details accessible; for members of the Tablighi Jamaat religious movement.
E Visa and E1 Visa (BIDA): Specific validity, max stay, and fee details not published on official investbangladesh.gov.bd pages; BIDA OSS portal (bidaquickserv.org) requires registration to access.
